title: Research Physicist — National Laboratory
setting: A government-funded national research laboratory (e.g. ANSTO, CSIRO, or a university research group)
companySize: Research group of 8–15 people within a larger facility of 500+
description: You work at a national laboratory conducting fundamental or applied physics research. Your days split between designing experiments, collecting data, analysing results, and writing papers. You collaborate with researchers worldwide, supervise postdocs and students, and present at international conferences.

schedule.activity: Arrive at the lab, check overnight experimental run logs
schedule.note: The experiment ran overnight collecting neutron scattering data

schedule.activity: Morning coffee and scan arXiv for new papers in your field
schedule.note: Two relevant papers posted overnight — flag for later reading

schedule.activity: Analyse overnight data — check for anomalies and extract preliminary results
schedule.note: The signal-to-noise looks promising but needs more statistics

schedule.activity: Group meeting — present preliminary results, discuss next steps
schedule.note: Your supervisor suggests extending the measurement to lower temperatures

schedule.activity: Work on a paper draft — writing the methods section and preparing figures
schedule.note: Target submission to Physical Review Letters

schedule.activity: Lunch with collaborators — discuss a joint proposal
schedule.note: Informal discussions often lead to the best ideas

schedule.activity: Reconfigure the experiment for the next measurement series
schedule.note: Change the sample, realign optics, run calibration checks

schedule.activity: Supervise a PhD student's analysis — help debug their fitting code
schedule.note: Teaching is a core part of the research physicist role

schedule.activity: Write code for a simulation that models the experiment
schedule.note: Comparing simulation to data helps identify systematic effects

schedule.activity: Set up the overnight experimental run and head home
schedule.note: The experiment runs unattended overnight, collecting data while you sleep

responsibilities: Analyse data and publish results in peer-reviewed journals
Write grant proposals to secure research funding
Supervise postdocs, PhD students, and honours students
Present at national and international conferences
Collaborate with researchers at other institutions
challenges: Securing ongoing research funding through competitive grants
Balancing experiment time, analysis, writing, and supervision
Publishing at the pace required for career progression
Equipment failures at critical moments in an experimental campaign
The pressure to produce novel results in a competitive field
rewards: The thrill of discovering something no one has ever seen before
Intellectual freedom to pursue fundamental questions
Working with brilliant colleagues from around the world
Training the next generation of physicists
Contributing to humanity's understanding of the universe
careerPath: PhD → Postdoctoral researcher → Research fellow → Senior researcher → Laboratory director
